An improved variant of the unsuccessful MiG-1, the MiG-3 was fast, agile, and complex to fly. It defended the Soviet Union during the beginning of Operation Barbarossa. This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the MiG-3 and contains various scenarios:

Moscow Summer Nights: Ju-88 bombers and a Bf-110C escort attack at night, forcing MiG-3 to be used as nightfighters

Soviet Naval Air Unit 402: MiG-3 and Bf-109F square off at high altitude.

Red Swarm: Escort Il-2 Sturmovik bombers to strike a Luftwaffe airfield. Bf-109F fighters continue to take off and replace losses.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – The Unmade
description:
Hailing from the island of Tzlid, close to the Shyish Nadir, the Unmade are a tribe of cannibal killers obsessed with pain and bodily mutilation. To these sadistic creatures, true worship of Chaos can be achieved only through the giving and receiving of agony.

Strike terror into the hearts of your foes with The Unmade! This Warcry warband set allows you to field these horrifying cultists in your skirmish battles, containing:

     9 multipart plastic The Unmade Miniatures: 1 Blissful One, 1 Joyous One, 3 Ascended Ones and 4 Awakened Ones
     An Unmade Abilities card, allowing you to unleash the distinctive and deadly battle tactics of this warband in your games
     5 Fighter Cards for keeping track of your rules at a glance, plus divider cards for tracking your tokens


What’s more, these warriors of Shyish are available for use in your Warhammer Age of Sigmar army thanks to a free downloadable warscroll.

This set is supplied with 4 x 25mm round bases, 3 x 28mm round bases, 1 x 32mm round base and a 1 x 40mm round base.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Splintered Fang
description:
The warriors of the Splintered Fang descend from the nomadic tribes of the great jungles of Invidia, and once worshipped the godbeast Nagendra as part of a pantheon of animalistic deities. Now, they worship the strange creatures known as the Coiling Ones, consuming searing toxins to prove their worthiness…

Battle through Warcry with speed, skill and deadly poisons with the Splintered Fang! This warband set allows you to field these serpentine warriors in your skirmish battles, containing:

     10 multipart plastic Splintered Fang Miniatures; 1 Trueblood, 1 Pureblood, 1 Serpent Caller, 2 Venombloods (with a choice of weapons for each), 4 Clearbloods, and 1 set of Serpents
     A Splintered Fang Abilities card, allowing you to unleash the distinctive and deadly battle tactics of this warband in your games
     6 Fighter Cards for keeping track of your rules at a glance, plus divider cards for tracking your tokens


What’s more, these warriors of Ghyran are available for use in your Warhammer Age of Sigmar army thanks to a free downloadable warscroll.

This set is supplied with 5 x 25mm round bases, 2 x 28mm round bases, and 3 x 32mm round base.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Cypher Lords
description:
Rulers of the city-state of Nochseed, the Cypher Lords are masters of illusion and deception. They have gazed into the dark heart of Chaos and embraced its infinite madness.

Unleash madness, mayhem and utter anarchy on the battlefields of Warcry with the Cypher Lords! This warband set allows you to field these mysterious saboteurs in your games, containing:

     8 multi-part plastic Cypher Lords miniatures - 1 Thrallmaster, 1 Luminate, 3 Mirrorblades and 3 Mindbound.
     A Cypher Lords abilities card, allowing you to master the strange and deadly tricks of the Cypher Lords in your battles.
     6 fighter cards for keeping track of your rules at a glance in games.


What’s more, these warriors of Hysh are available for use in your Warhammer Age of Sigmar army thanks to a free warscroll available in the Downloads tab.

This set is supplied with 1x 32mm round base, 4x 28mm rounds bases, and 3x 25mm round bases.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Gloomspite Gitz
description:
Cruel, cunning and treacherous, the Gloomspite Gitz infest the dank places of the Mortal Realms like a mouldering fungus. Though anarchic creatures by their very nature, when they unite under a common cause they are capable of wholescale destruction.

Spread anarchy and mayhem across the Eightpoints with this Warcry card pack! Combined with the Core Rules and your miniatures, this set allows you to command a warband of anarchic Gloomspite Gitz in your skirmish battles. An abilities card allows you to use deadly in-game tactics and powers, while a set of fighter cards provide profiles for Boingrot Bounderz, Squig Hoppers, Stabbas, Shootas, Squig Herders and Cave Squigs.

This set contains an abilities card in English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Chinese, Japanese, and Russian, as well as several fighter cards. Please note that a copy of the Warcry Core Book is needed to use this set.

—description from the publisher

An expansion for the Ghostel board game, including Legends of the horror genre.

—description from the designer

GoYe combines strategic gameplay with Seventh-Day Adventist history, identity, and mission. Work with your friends to tell the world by spreading the gospel across a world map of 58 conferences and 10 divisions.  Collect Total Member Involvement by investing your resources in mission trips, spiritual gifts, churches, and offices. Once every conference has a church, the second coming ends the game, and the player with the most TMI wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Table Air Combat: Mosquito Fighter variant
description:
Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface. Each ruleset is a self-contained game including cut-out miniatures of one aircraft and a set of rules. All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.

Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.

TAC is designed for portability: the entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the British Mosquito fighter-bomber variant.

In addition to the Mosquito FB VI (fighter-bomber variant), this set also includes:


     Mosquito Mk. XVII Tsetse (anti-shipping variant with 57mm cannon)
     Mosquito NF VI (radar-equipped night fighter variant)
     V-1 Buzz bomb
     ground target for Amiens Prison
     Ship targets for verpostenboot, sperrbrecher, and Type VIIC U-boat

This set contains four scenarios.

Intruder:  Night-fighter versions of the Mosquito raid a Luftwaffe airfield at night. Returning Ju-88 bombers are prey, but a Bf-110G night fighter defends.

Operation Jericho: Free French prisoners from the Gestapo by knocking down the prison walls. Fw-190A fighters from a nearby airfield counter-attack.

Operation Crossbow: Intercept V-1 flying bombs before they strike British targets.

Mosquito Bites: Tsetse anti-shipping variants of the Mosquito attack a surfaced U-boat as it transitions though the Bay of Biscay.

As a merchant, your goal in Age of Fairs is to accumulate the most reputation and money by giving your customers the best combination of change.

Each round, players will be greeted by a new queue of customers. How these customers are treated will determine who will be the winner.

There are many customers who pay for your wares with high value coins which, of course, need to be exchanged for change. By giving the best combination of change to the customer, your reputation will rise as customers walk away happy to be free of the annoyance of jangling coins in their pockets. If you give them too many coins, your reputation will fall as your customer is angry with the great weight of coins you've sent them away with.

Though we live in a world with convenient electronic payment by credit card, much of the history of mankind has been faced with this problem: how to manage money held in hand.
—user summary

boardgame: Table Air Combat: Bf-110G Night Fighter
description:
Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface. Each ruleset is a self-contained game including cut-out miniatures of one aircraft and a set of rules. All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.

Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.

TAC is designed for portability: the entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

The Bf-110 was ineffective as an escort fighter but served as an exceptional night fighter. The twin-engine design allowed radar set to be installed in the nose.  This was operated by the second crewman, who also operated the tail gun. This set contains the BF-110G4, the definitive Zerstroyer night fighter. Also included is the Bf-110G2, a daylight heavy fighter which trades the high-drag radar antenna for a higher maximum speed.

This set is a self-contained rulebook with miniatures for the Bf-110G and four scenarios.

Viermot: Engage a daylight raid of B-17 Flying Fortresses with air-to-air rockets. P-47 Thunderbolts escort the bombers.

Schrade Music: Attack a formation of Lancaster bombers at night using upward-firing cannon.

Jabo vs. Jabo:  Engage Il-2 and Yak-7 while protecting tanks on the Russian Front.

Zahme Sau:  Bf-110 and Bf-109G night fighters use spotlights to engage a regenerating stream of Lancaster bombers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Table Air Combat: Mosquito Bomber variant

This set contains five scenarios.

Oslo: A treetop-level surgical strike against the Gestapo HQ in Norway, upon request of the Norwegian government-in-exile.  Fw-190A fighters defend.

Operation Oyster: Brave heavy flak and defending Fw-190A fighters to bomb an electronic research plant in Nazi-occupied Holland.

Schott Glass: Heavy flak protects Jena in Germany, where Mosquitos need to hit two factories.

Pathfinder: Mosquitos use the cover of night to drop illumination flares on a target, paving the way for an imminent Lancaster strike. A Bf-110G night fighter hunt.

Photoreconnaissance: Armed with only cameras, a Mosquito element must fly over locations to photograph and then return to get the film developed.  Bf-109F fighters defend, while a Spitfire V provides close escort.

—description from the publisher

The B-25H was an armoured strafer version of the B-25. The bombardier's position was replaced with a 75mm field cannon, the glazed nose was replaced with armour plating, and an additional four .50 machine guns were installed. B-25H aircraft engaged in anti-shipping and low-level strafing.
 This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the B-25H and contains four scenarios that may reference other aircraft:

Destroyer: A single B-25H element engages a gun duel against a Japanese destroyer.

Convoy: Attack a Japanese supply convoy.

Wewak: Fight A6M5 Zero fighters while skip-bombing Japanese shipping

Rabaul: Strafe a heavily-defended Japanese airfield using parafrag bombs. Heavy AAA fire and continually-spawning A6M5 Zero fighters threaten to overwhelm the strafers.

New rules include: 

     AAA positions: Land-based guns that guard targets.
     Flak suppression:  Heavy maching gun fire can temporarily stop a ship or AAA position from firing.
     Parafrags:  Small fragmentation bombs that are used at low altitudes.


New targets include:

     Kagero-class Japanese destroyer
     Japanese cargo ship
     Japanese airfield
     AAA positions

Frontier Wars: Expansion France/Japan brings two very different factions — Japan and France — into Frontier Wars, while also allow for games with up to six players. This expansion also includes a new type of tile (double tiles) and new additional scenarios.

Adidoku is a new generation of puzzles with a fun mix of characters, places and phrases. Guess the connections and stimulate your brain !

To resolve Adidoku (puzzle), distribute on a table all the pieces showing the "Arts" tiles side. Connect the pieces by checking the words and phrases that seem related. For example: connect "Freddy" with "Mercury" or "Frida" with "Kahlo".

"Palabretas" Mini game included. In Palabretas you compete to make 4 words before the others and achieve the highest number of points! You can use each image as one or several letters. Use an ICE to create dICE, or EAT to create mEAT, or BOot to create BOss. Increase your points with colors, hearts or clovers.

A game without turns and unexpected combinations!

Sudoku and board games are combined in Numberplace City.

You are the president of a construction company that undertook the construction of an undersea city. We will build the buildings at the same time as our rivals, but we cannot build the same building in the same column, the same row, and the same block.

After you have built the minimum number of buildings, you can use various effects. Some of the effects include taking the start player marker, spinning the board, or winning the game.

Well, who is the best architect?

Iris is a two player abstract strategy game that is played on a "hexhex" board (a hexagonal shaped board made with hexagonal cells).  An Iris board has rainbow colored perimeter cells and gray interior cells. Boards of sizes 5-8 cells per side are recommended.  (Size 5 is recommended for beginners.) Player 1 plays with black stones and Player 2 plays with white stones.

On the very first turn, Player 1 plays a SINGLE black stone to any gray cell.  Thereafter, starting with Player 2 on his/her first turn, each player plays TWO stones per turn, subject to the following restrictions:

1) If the first stone of a turn is played on a colored perimeter cell, then the second stone MUST be played on the corresponding same-colored cell on the opposite side of the hexhex board.  (In the case of a corner cell, the corresponding same-colored cell is the opposite corner cell.)

2) If the first stone of a turn is played on a gray interior cell, then the second stone MUST be played on any empty non-adjacent gray cell.  (If all remaining empty gray cells are adjacent to the first stone, then the second stone is forfeited.)

When the board is full or when both players consecutively pass, the game ends and players score their groups of stones.  A group’s score is equal to the number of colored cells that it occupies.  The player with the highest scoring group wins. If scores are tied, then players ignore their highest scoring group, and compare the highest scoring of their remaining groups.  If still tied, then players compare their next highest scoring groups, etc., until the tie is broken.  (Despite there being an even number of colored cells, for subtle reasons having to do with board geometry, it turns out that draws are impossible.)

(A note on the game's name: "Iris" was the Greek goddess of the rainbow.)



Honors


     Iris was a finalist in the Best Combinatorial 2-Player Game Contest of 2019







-description from designer

A simple roll-and-move game designed for children who are unable to read, but are able to tell different colors apart. Gameplay resembles a simplified version of Candyland: Players select a colored movement pawn, then take turns rolling a die with various colors represented on it to see where they move their pawn.  The player who reaches the end of the path first wins.
Four new buildings to add to the game, distributed by the publisher: Sushi bar, Temple, Cornucopia and Construction site.

NFL Trade$ Card Game is a fast paced card game where the goal is to grab the best combination of top picks as they rush by. Score big by making combos of player positions, teams and division. Acquire the top pick before your opponent to build a higher scoring team. Gather the most points and become the ultimate Trade$ winner!

—description from the designer

When 2 dogs broke into the Boneyard, the Skeleton's bones were scattered and lost. Compete with your friends to find and rebuild the Skeletons while the Dogs roam the Boneyard looking for something to chew on.

The game takes place on a 2 layer, 4x4 grid of tiles. On your turn you will flip tiles and look for Bones. There are 10 Skeletons and each has 3 Bones. You will earn points by combining the Bones in matching or mixed sets, and in sets of 2 or 3 Bones. End your turn by moving the Dog tiles and blocking the next player from specific bones. By the end of the game, the player with the highest score of assembled Skeletons wins.

—description from the designer
Additional chip for Letter Jam, allowing 9-letter words.

description: Alternative village sheet that was originally given as a promotional item at BerlinCon 2019, but has been made available afterwards as a free download on the website of Portal games.

The Gobblers family has organized their traditional hide-and-seek party! The mums hide the teenagers, and the teenagers hide the babies! You will need to rely on memory and observation to help you find them in Gobblers Family Mix. Flip a card and be the first to deduce the missing Gobblers and to find it! Beware of the Great-Aunt and Great-Uncle who are the king and queen of troubles...

—description from the publisher

The players have to get their pawn from space 1 to space 300. Movement is determined by a spinner. The spinner indicates two numbers, wich are added to determine the number of spaces, the pawn has to move forward. If a pawn gets on a space with a picture and an arithmetical task (based on the number of the space), the meeple has to be moved forwards or backwards accordingly. The first player to reach space 300 wins the game.

Six promo cards sent to retailers and subscribers with the release of Curse of the Crimson Throne.

Ally P5: Asyra
Armor P3: Corpse Plate

Weapon P3: The Tall Knife

It's not a party without a pinata! In this exciting and colorful Pop! Pop! Pinata game, players take turns hitting the button on the base to send the disk spinning upward toward the donkey pinata. A gentle hit releases a few prizes for the player to collect. But here's the catch! A hard hit will pop open the pinata releasing all of the prizes, but that also means that all players can race to collect the prizes. The player with the most prizes wins!

The Pop! Pop! Pinata game comes with over 30 colorful plastic prizes. It's easy to load them by lifting the donkey's saddle and pouring the prizes inside. Players take turns hitting the party button on the base, sending the spinning disc up to the bottom of the pinata. If a player hits the disc gently, a few prizes will pop out and the player whose turn it is can add those to their cup. When a player hits the pinata harder, it will pop open! That means more prizes, but it also means that now everyone gets to race to put the prizes into their cups! Who will end up with the most prizes once the pinata is empty?

—description from the publisher

Race your opponent as you attempt to catapult all the squishy fish through the center of the game board. But you better work fast… the first player to launch all fish to their opponent’s side wins. It will require speed, accuracy and concentration if you want to be the king of the ocean.

Honesty Stone is a bluffing game for 2-5 players, a party game to enjoy with family and friends.

Players must be honest without telling a lie - if they are revealed to be  a liar by other players by using honesty stone they would be out of the game.

The game is simple and easy - players have three Jewels each, and each turn players guess the color of another player's Jewel. Different color Jewels have different effects, allowing players to reject a guess, exchange stones, or make more powerful guesses.  Using an effect gives your opponents clues as to what color stone you have.  Assuming you are telling the truth.

The player who keeps the color of their Jewels hidden until the game end wins.

Based on the designer's book, Whispers of Hemmingbird.

Have you always wanted glory, wealth and victories? Here is your time!

Fantasy Crystals is coming, and with it comes a plethora of heroes you can lead to triumph in epic clashes! Seize the opportunity to embark on a fantastic land full of riches and irrepressible rivals. Don't hesitate. Stand up to fight! The winner takes all!

Fantasy Crystals is a card game for two to four people with a dedicated solo mode. Players choose their champions from among the wealth-hungry adventurers to measure themselves in constant duels for crystals and glory. Players must use cunning and cool calculation to create their unique tactics and challenge the world.

Everything you need to play our game from two to four players, as well as a solo mode! The game includes 2 identical decks, which differ only in symbols. A 2-player game requires only 1 deck to play, for a 3- and 4-player game you need 2 decks.

—description from the publisher

Monkey King Chess by Peter Gelman is a chess variant with a main board representing Earth and two small boards representing clouds, 44 squares total. In it, the Monkey King fights the Goblin King in a theme with oriental influences.

The Earth board includes a river which influences play. Forces are asymmetrical, representing mythological forces including sea dragons, celestials and mountains.

The object of the game for the Monkey King player is to move his Pilgrim off the opposite side of the board; a winning move would be any Pilgrim move from row 8 off the board. The object of the game for the Goblin King player is to checkmate the Pilgrim. As with Sittuyin (Burmese Chess), a move that causes stalemate is illegal.

A piece requires the ability to fly to move to or from the Clouds. A few types of disk-faces have this ability. The ability to fly does not imply any additional movement ability or limitations. The fliers are: the six Monkey Kings, the two Goblin Generals, the two Lancers of each side, and the one Celestial of each side. Only a flier can fly to or from or across the Cloud squares. No piece may make a capture when they fly to or from a cloud. Furthermore, a disk may only fly to a cloud square from the magic river square associated with it. The disk may land on either of the two squares of that cloud, so long as that square is empty. No piece may make a capture when they fly to or from a cloud, including flight from one cloud to the other. A disk starting movement on a Cloud square must note the color of the square first; it may fly to any empty square on the Earth board of the same color, or the other Clouds square of the same color if it is empty.

Except for the disks that represent the Pilgrim and the Goblin King, all of the disks are two-sided, with a different chess piece showing on either side. If you start a turn with a disk standing on any magic river square, you may flip the disk and simultaneously move it according to that new game piece's abilities. To put it another way, a disk standing on a magic river square has the ability to move as the disk on either side of its two faces. A disk may not flip if it is not on magic river square.

The Goblin King player cannot flip a disk if the Goblin King disk is not on Earth board (thus before initial placement, or after its capture, means the Goblin King player cannot flip disks). The Monkey King player cannot flip disks unless he/she has initially placed the Pilgrim disk on the board.

The two sides' pieces start the game roughly, but not exactly symmetrical. For the most part, even when identical on the front side, Red and Blue disks flip for different transformations.

There are twelve types of disks in the game: Pawn, Sea Serpent, Monkey King, Knight, Lancers, Goblin General, Whirling Monkey Fists, Celestial (a different type on each side), Goblin King, and Buddhist Pilgrim.

The game starts with the boards empty. Starting with the Goblin King player, each player takes turn either introducing a disk on their half of the Earth board, or moving a disk they previously had placed. Players may not introduce disks in this manner on any Cloud square.

The disks have a front and a back. Players may only place disks with the front side showing. The back side of the disks cannot appear unless the piece stands on a magic river square and flips it.

All pieces that can capture move as they capture except the Sea Serpent. The Pilgrim and the Red Celestial cannot capture. Brave Whirling Monkey Fists is an off-board counter, not a normal type of chess piece. Its design intention is to help compensate for the helpless Pilgrim piece.

The classic tale "Journey to the West" inspired the theme and function of this chess variant. Chess game influences include Sittuyin, Moscow King Chess, Shogi, and standard Western chess.

—description from the designer

Introduction:

You are a conspiracy theorist.
Your goal is to get the highest number of followers on your conspiracy blog, no matter the cost.
Create attention-grabbing blog headlines, influence world events, and sabotage your competition.

Summary:

Cardspiracy is a 4 to 6 player social-strategy card game where players compete to attract the most followers by creating viral headlines on their conspiracy blog.

Headlines can be augmented or undermined by events and actions. Prior to scoring, players vote for their favourite conspiracy and assign followers to reward creativity. If they play to the strengths of their secret identity, it could mean a big boost of followers at the end of the game.
Can you grow the biggest following, or are you doomed to obscurity?

Features:

     Two Games in One - A simplified Party-Mode focused on building conspiracies removes strategic elements from the game to allow expanded player count (4 - 8 players)
     High replayability - With over 500,000 Conspiracy Combinations
     Combined Social and Strategy - Gameplay rewards creativity and encourages long term planning


—description from the publisher

You and your fellow Tin Dragon’s seek to accumulate the largest treasure hoard. But only one dragon may claim the title of the Mightiest of the Tin Dragons and rule the skies.

The game is playable with two to four players.  Play time is about 20 minutes.

Assault the six Keeps that protect the riches of the nearby kingdoms, defeat their defenses, raid their coffers, duel your foes, and avoid the pesky Knight that seeks to slay a dragon. When the realm has no more riches to offer the dragon with the largest hoard will be crowned the winner.

Tin Dragons are named as such from the silvery blue color of their hide and metallic scales. They possess extremely sharp claws and can let out a roar that only the most stalwart adventurer could resist fleeing from in fear.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Ironjawz
description:
The Ironjawz are the biggest and hardest orruks of all. Clad in pig-iron armour and swinging enormous, bone-breaking weapons, they wreak untold devastation across the realms.

Destroy, pillage and smash your way through the battlefields of Warcry with this card pack! Combined with the Core Rules and your miniatures, this set allows you to command a warband of brutal Ironjawz in your skirmish battles. An abilities card lets you use deadly in-game tactics and powers, while a set of fighter cards provide profiles for Brutes and 'Ardboys.

This set contains an abilities card in English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Chinese, Japanese, and Russian, as well as several fighter cards. Please note that a copy of the Warcry Core Book is needed to use this set.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Battleplan Cards
description:
Generate awesome battleplans in moments with these decks of battleplan cards! With these three decks, you’ll be able to create new scenarios for your warbands in mere moments, featuring a host of different deployment maps, objectives, twists and more. This set contains:


     36 deployment cards


Shows different examples of where to set-up your warbands


     36 victory cards


Details 36 different victory conditions


     36 twist cards


Special rules that are used for the duration of each battle.

Combine these with terrain cards, and you’ll be able to play millions of potential battleplans – no two need be the same!

Please note – you'll need a copy of the Warcry Core Book to use this set, plus any of the Terrain decks featured in Warcry Ravaged Lands sets or the starter set. These cards are also included in the Warcry Starter Set – if you have a copy, you won't need these!

—description from the publisher

Hall Pass is a family game where players are students trying to skip school by moving their pawns and bluffing other players to avoid being put into detention.

Each player has seven large pawns, and on their turn they may take one of two actions. First, they may move one of their pawns pawn from its home room orthagonally in a straight line as far as they want, stopping when they run into another pawn; or second, take a pawn that is in detention and return it to one of their home rooms.

Each pawn is numbered from 1 to 7 on the bottom, and it can only legally move exactly that many spaces. Any player can demand to see a just-moved pawn's hall pass (the number) and, if it moved either more or fewer spaces than that number, it is sent to detention.

If a player can move a pawn out of the school through any home room of another player, it is moved to the getaway car in their player color. The first player to move all seven of their pawns to the getaway car wins the game.

The first set for Legendary Endeavors CCG. Nightmare set is a survival horror collectible card game. Bringing together human survivors, demons, monsters, creatures, zombies and more.
The game play is fast and done with dice to add the randomness of combat. By creating decks or using one of several methods the Players are tasked with being the last Player with Character Cards alive. Game Play usually takes 20-30 minutes with the Starter Deck. The overlaying goal is to use all cards until only one player is standing.

—description from the designer

The Warsaw Uprising was a major World War II operation, in the summer of 1944, by the Polish underground resistance, led by the Home Army, to liberate Warsaw from German occupation. The uprising was timed to coincide with the retreat of the German forces from Poland ahead of the Soviet advance. While approaching the eastern suburbs of the city, the Red Army temporarily halted combat operations, enabling the Germans to regroup and defeat the Polish resistance and to raze the city in reprisal. The Uprising was fought for 63 days with little outside support. It was the single largest military effort taken by any European resistance movement during World War II.

Game covers whole uprising, since the first to the last days of fights, giving players a chance to see if there was any way to win this possibly pointless battle. German player controls Whermacht, SS and eastern collaboration forces, while Polish player controls all the major underground resistance units as well as pro-soviet Polish People Army that tried to help the uprising.

Game is drived by command points - each player has a limited number of these. Players spend command points to perform certain actions like movement, attack, resupply, introduce reinforcements, pacify, ruin or build the barrickades. There also cards depicting various events that happend throughout the uprising.

description:
A limited edition suit of six cards. The incredible hat was created by our community.

Winning words: Fishbowl, turtle, sloth, unicorn, teapot

—description from the publisher.

The $100,000 Pyramid DVD game is fast-paced and easy-to-play - just pop it into your DVD player and try to top the Pyramid!

Like real game show contestants, you shout out clues for your teammates while they try to guess their way through a maze of challenging and humorous categories. Whether you're giving clues or guessing answers, you've got to think fast and talk fast to reach the Winners Circle!

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Echoes of Death 2
description:
Distant memories, echoes of heroic melodies shape the survivors.

Echoes of Death 2 includes 4 new survivors that embody memories from another world. These memories can be unlocked by reaching unique milestones in the story of your campaign.

This set includes prototypes of Strain System mechanics. Add your Strain Milestone cards to your game, if you reach their unique conditions, your gameplay changes forever. A new fighting art born from distant dreams is permanently added to your game.

The Echoes of Death 2 custom box includes:

1 x Cleric Survivor
1 x Ranger Survivor
1 x Gladiator Survivor with scenic base insert
1 x Bard Survivor with scenic base insert
2 x Hard plastic 30mm insert
4 x Hard plastic 30mm bases
4 x Large matte print of the original concept artwork on heavy card stock
1 x prototype Strain Card rules sheet for adding to your campaign
4 x Strain Milestone cards
4 x Strain Fighting Arts
1 x Strain Fighting Arts card divider
1 x Strain Milestone card divider

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – White Speaker Sword Hunter
description: This is a set of three promo cards included with The White Speaker Sword Hunter, a miniature that is not part of the game. There is one Hunt Event with an associated Settlement Event and Gear card.

The Ringtail Vixen white box includes:

1 x Hard plastic Ringtail Vixen
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round base & insert
Large matte print of the original concept artwork on heavy card stock
Character art card
Bookmark rules. Play with this to add the Ringtail Vixen to your Kingdom Death: Monster campaign

Once, a couple of despondent survivors wandered into the darkness. Following faint cries. they arrived at a river of blood. Floating on its languid current, a baby in a basket of woven fur. Forsaking oblivion, the pair unexpectedly returned to their home with the infant in their arms. Strong and preternaturally fast, the child grew into a wild, beautiful woman bearing strange features like needle sharp teeth and strong fingernails that grew at an alarming rate.

Finding an affinity with the fierce fox monsters that haunt her dreams, she crafted her armor with clawed feet, a hood with vulpine ears, and fox tails.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Piper vs. Ripsnarl
description:
One is a skilled hunter, the eagle-eyed sniper who never misses a shot. The other is a fearsome werewolf, who is both a formidable villain and a literal party animal.

You've seen these two tangle in Battle for Greyport, our cooperative deckbuilding game, but now they're coming to the tavern to join the party as our 48th and 49th characters for The Red Dragon Inn.

Piper is a brilliant marksman who can hit any target with ease. She tends to work alone, but joins our heroes on occasion when that "long distance touch" is needed. Folks around the tavern know that her wit is as sharp as her arrows, so they have extra reason to stay on her good side!

The Good: Piper never misses a shot.
The Bad: This applies to drinking contests as well as archery.

Ripsnarl is always ready for a howling good time... when he's not in his scrawny human form. When he's a werewolf, he becomes a literal party animal. The rest of the time you'll find him fuming and plotting his revenge. He always finds ways to transform again, even when the moon wanes.

The Bad: Ripsnarl keeps his claws razor sharp.
The Worse: ... and Piper has the scars to prove it.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This is a set of items for Blood Rage that were only available during the Blood Rage Digital Kickstarter campaign in 2018.

Among the exclusives there were:

     new versions of the 4 Monsters only available in the original Kickstarter campaign (Mountain Giantess, Garm, Seer Troll, Wolfwoman)
     3 brand new monsters (Ymir, Hildisvíni and Nídhöggr)
     the brand-new all-female Stag Clan (including their Mystics and Sculpted Clan Tokens)
     24 Cards to add a 5th Player into the board game
     Golden Gods of Asgard (deluxe version of the classic expansion with re-sculpted oversized golden figures, and now including Hel - the new God of Asgard to carry on the original game effect of Hili, a previously released exclusive promo)
     new versions of 3 Sculpted Tokens (Doom, Saga and First Player)
     new Plastic Pillage Tokens
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Nebrodensis: the tale of Cremete and the Wolf Cristaldii
description:
In the East of the island of Sicily lived a man named Cremete (similar to Radagast of Lotr), he controlled the animals in the lost woods; this man managed to impress King Roger II (1095 - 1154). In the west of the island of Sicily there lived a wolf (now extinct): the Canis Lupus Cristaldii, a large and fast, lethal blond wolf. This is true the story of a Wolf and a man who lived in the deep European Middle Ages: do you want to be part of it? Nebrodensis is the name of the Sicilian mountains and in this enchanted island the game develops on 8 abstract games 1vs1 (or 1vs2) or other in 8 solitaire games; symmetrical and asymmetrical, strategic and aleatory, german and american, nocturnal or diurnal, pushed into a psychological wood where only fangs and magic win. The only way to survive is to become an extinct Wolf or a magical Hermit really existed: do you accept the challenge or are you too afraid of the moon?

—description from the publisher

Unpredictable up to the last hand, Electoral College Combat combines strategy and chance as two players amass Electoral College votes while navigating Fake News, Russian collusion, recounts, celebrity endorsements, and swing states to become President of the United States.

There are 56 cards:  50 state cards and Washington DC, whose value is reflected in their electoral vote count, and five wild cards.  28 cards are dealt to each player who take turns laying five cards face-down and sight unseen from the top of their stacks.  Each player turns over one card.  The player with the higher card can take that card and any other or the lower card and two of the remaining face-down cards.  Opponent gets the remaining cards.

Electoral College Combat is designed to simulate the wild swings and uncertainties of election night.  A sound strategy--like a well-run campaign--does not guarantee results.  That's all part of the fun.  While not a gambling game, Electoral College Combat finds its roots in video slots/poker where the wild cards activate bonus rounds.

Each wild card has specific rules.  A player who turns over a second wild card in the same hand wins the remaining cards.

There are twelve hands.  In the 11th and 12th hands each player has only three cards remaining.  When their turn arrives, players must add two cards of the cards they've collected to their hand:  their highest card and another of their choice, shuffle the cards, and place them in front of their opponent.  Play continues as before. When play is concluded, the player with 270 or more electoral college votes wins the presidency.  2nd prize?  Hindsight and book deal.  Ghost writer available upon request.

—description from the publisher

Based on the already popular games Doubt, Cheat, and Bullshit, BS Button Game is a shedding game for 2-6 players.  The deck contains 52 standard playing cards featuring original art, two jokers, and two bureaucrats.  Any player can call a bluff by pushing the BS Button which flashes red and calls bullshit with one of 60 random celebrity impressions.

The joker and bureaucrat cards allow play with as few as two players.

Jokers and bureaucrats are wild.  Additionally, any player who holds a bureaucrat and has incorrectly called a bluff or has been called on a bad bluff can play the bureaucrat and override the call.  Whoever plays the bureaucrat redetermines the next card and plays it.

Play continues until one player discards every card while avoiding a valid call of bullshit.

—description from the publisher

BS Button Game Expansion Pack offers a new spin on shedding games like Doubt, Cheat, and Bullshit.  It includes a sound button, True Blue, which flashes bright blue and calls bullshit 70 different ways, 52 standard playing cards with original art, and a new wild card, the activist.

The addition of the activist card allows stand alone play with three to six players.  BS Button Game Expansion Pack can also be combined with BS Button Game to accommodate up to twelve players.

The activist is wild and can be played to call a rank out of sequence and force an opponent to discard that card or to bluff.  Players have the option of hitting the BS Button anytime they suspect a bluff.

The first player who discards every card while avoiding a valid call of bullshit wins.

—description from the publisher
Using the Tip-Toi system, hunt for missing animal tiles by playing a variety of minigames put forward by the dragon.

boardgame: Iron Helm: Adventure Pack #1 – Warren of Despair
description:
A set of 18 cards to move up to level 2

Warren of Despair is the 1st Adventure Pack for Iron Helm that adds all new Loot, Skills, Trappings, Plots, a new Boss to fight, and a new Character to play as. You will need to own the base game of Iron Helm to play this expansion.

Warren of Despair is a level two Adventure Pack meaning it is to be incorporated with the base game as your second adventure if you survive the base game.

Contents

1 Boss Card - 1 Character Card - 1 Skill Card - 2 Trappings Cards - 4 Loot Cards - 3 Plot Cards - 5 Enemy Cards - 1 Map Card

boardgame: Iron Helm: Adventure Pack #2 – Blackened Burrow
description:
Blackened Burrow is the 2nd Adventure Pack for Iron Helm that adds all new Loot, Skills, Trappings, Plots, a new Boss to fight, and a new Character to play as. You will need to own the base game of Iron Helm to play this expansion.

Blackened Burrow is a level three Adventure Pack meaning it is to be incorporated with the base game as your third adventure if you survive the base game and a level 2 Adventure Pack.

1 Boss Card - 1 Character Card - 1 Skill Card - 2 Trappings Cards - 4 Loot Cards - 3 Plot Cards - 5 Enemy Cards - 1 Map Card

--description from designer

The second expansion to Euclio, Euclio Tricky! adds five new scoring boards and accompanying suit cards to make your games even more varied and interesting.

These new boards are a bit more... tricky, and therefore recommended for Euclio veterans, but all retain the general simplicity of play Euclio has always had.

Euclio Tricky! adds a risky new chip type, more calls to have points on hand, and a surprise behind door number 2! Get ready to sweat bullets staring down these tricky new suits. Simply add to your Euclio box and experience a new mixture of gameplay possibilities!

—description from the designer

Raging Kingdoms is a medieval-themed real-time strategy (RTS) inspired board game.

Command one of history’s great kingdoms and lead it to victory through skillful economic development, technological advancement, and, of course, the art of war!

Raging Kingdoms is a game for one to six players. A typical game takes 90 to 120 minutes.

Win by conquering all enemy empires, or by leaving them in awe of your Wonder Victory. Each turn, you'll roll a special die for your action moves. Make the most of your action moves to march closer to your goal.


     Create new units - villagers to gather resources, scouts to discover the hidden map, or swordsmen, archers and knights to crush threatening neighbors.
     Construct new buildings - camps to exploit resources, blacksmiths to equip your army, and towers to defend against aggressive opponents.
     Research new technologies to help your empire reach new heights.
     Devise the perfect attack to conquer new land and show your enemies your rage!


At the end of your turn, collect the resources your villagers have gathered for you and start planning your next moves!

Highlights:

     Immersive: Play as exciting historical civilizations like the Romans, Vikings, Mongols, and more!
     RTS Appeal: Intuitive for fans of classic medieval real-time strategy (RTS) games!
     Replayability: Newly generated map to discover each game, exciting game variants, single player and multiplayer campaigns!


Raging Kingdoms delivers in a tabletop game all the best parts of classic RTS video games, like:

     A randomly generated map that players discover, making each game different
     Managing the gathering and stockpiling of 4 resources
     Finding the perfect balance between reinvesting in your economy, strengthening your military, and researching new technology
     Choosing which military units will be most effective in which battle situations
     Strategically deciding when, where, and how to strike your opponents
     Tactically commanding units to lead them to victory


—description from the publisher

This book brings together several related exercises in game design. The game of Tetrad itself is the product of an effort to devise a simple game of abstract strategy for four players . . . .

The Tetrad board was designed with several considerations in mind: to provide a layout well suited to a four-way game, to accommodate a wide variety of traditional games, and to be compact enough to fit on the cover of the book. . . .

The TETRAD playing field consists of 120 octagonal spaces arranged in an octagonal pattern. This pattern is printed on both covers. The game is played on one side of the closed book. The playing pieces and dice are stored inside of the book.

The game of TETRAD is designed to be played by exactly four people.

—description from the book

Each player begins the game with six pieces of the same color. These are arranged in a row along one side of the board.

At the center of the board is a group of four spaces. These are each marked with a symbol. The objective of the game is to group four of one's playing pieces at the center. The first player whose color occupies all four of these spaces at the same time, wins the game.

The players take turns casting the dice and moving their pieces. A player begins a turn by casting the two dice. The score of the dice is the total number of steps that the player's pieces may be moved in that turn. One step is the movement of one piece from one space into the next. The pieces can be moved vertically or horizontally, but not diagonally. Moving your piece onto a space that is already occupied replaces your opponents color with your own.

The TETRAD board can be used to play many other games; instructions for various and adapted games are included in the book.

—user summary

Dimes is a party game that tests your ability to read the minds of your friends.

Using a Dimes card you'll present a friend with an either/or question. All the remaining players will try to guess which item that player prefers. Players are rewarded for successfully reading other players' minds.

The game is easily adapted as a drinking game and is ideal for party/social settings

—description from the publisher

This game was manufactured out of three different types of wood: Walnut, Pear and Acorn which have different colours thus making distinctive tokens. The name NUBIAH results out of the woods german names 'NUss BIrne AHorn'.

The playing board is a grid of 5x5 stones. Every second stone is fixed leaving a movable row of neutral tokens between them. This results in 4 moveable rows on the board.

Each player gets a total of 9 tokens of which 3 are unnumbered and the rest has a number between 1 and 6. The players start shoving their tokens in the gaming board and try to push out the opponent's already placed stones. The clue about this is, that you only can insert a stone in a row if your total score supersedes your opponent's.

If you manage to put all your tokens in the board you win.

—user summary

Goodbye Trivia, hello D.I.N.K. game. The game of the 1990's is here! Introducing new and exciting concepts. Guaranteed to known your socks off! You're given the opportunity to become a real life D.I.N.K. created by strategic moves, lucky dice rolling, and plain ol' good fun. It's exciting and fast paced.
—description from the publisher

Look out Yuppies, the D.I.N.K.S. have arrived! The Baby Boomers second generation is here! Once family-bound, now striving for Double Income No Kids. The combination of two incomes allows D.I.N.K.s to live the upper class lifestyle. . . .

The object of the D.I.N.K. Game is to live and acquire materialistic objects like a real live D.I.N.K. The Double Income will allow this between you and your spouse, by playing in teams and working together. Therefore the winner of the game is the first team to acquire the complete the D.I.N.K. Checklist, accumulating at least 40 points and acquiring $250,000 in the bank.

May also be played in the S.I.N.K. version (SINGLE INCOME NO KIDS). Same rules apply but there is only a single income.
—from a note on the inside of the box

D.I.N.K. = Double Income No Kids

Are you thinking what I'm thinking? You'll never know unless you play BUZZWORDS!

A challenging word game of first thoughts and second guesses. See how well you can zero in on a given category and determine its most obvious BUZZWORDS. Then find out how well you and your friends can think alike by guessing each others BUZZWORDS.

—description from the box

… SEVENEL is the future! SEVENEL has all those multiple choice questions everyone prefers. SEVENEL has no dice and we offer each player the opportunity to "Jump-In" on other people's questions. In addition, SEVENEL has more strategy than we can possibly explain. Using 7 different playing surfaces we have eliminated that dizzy feeling as well as "board boredom". Finally, if you do fall asleep, your opponents will be thrilled because they will surely benefit!

These are a few of the advantages the future holds for you with SEVENEL. We want you to experience the challenges we have discovered while playing the most versatile game of our time. And, there is no right or wrong way to play SEVENEL - you create your own version each time you play.

As you see, the history of trivia games promises to be much better as . . .

"night after night
        we play into the future!"

—description from the publisher

Increase vocabulary and word skills with WORDMASTER, a board game for players from early school age through adult.

Players earn points by correctly spelling, defining, identifying the part of speech and using the word on each card. Six degrees of challenge and achievement are built into color-coded cards. For individual or team play. (Play may be limited by time or number of turns.)

The Home Version of WORDMASTER is designed to allow participation by all family members, with three separate words on each card. These three groupings are for: children under 12, teens and adults.

School versions of WORDMASTER are designed for use in all grade levels.

Additional Word Card sets are available for different occupational specialities and special interests. The unique magnetic game board and magnetic playing pieces facilitate play and may be utilized with any Word Card set.

WORDMASTER has been developed from appropriate curriculum sources to provide a game that is both fun and exciting to play, as well as educationally rewarding.

description:
Cannibal Collective is a party game for people who have a strong stomach.

The premise of the game is simple, you have been invited to the 58th Annual Cannibal Collective initiation dinner and as part of the initiation you have to collect the very best bodyparts to comprise your meal.

The first person to collect 1 of each of the 7 body part cards is the winner.

But be warned! There are many other aspects at play! These include The Platypus of Doom, Mystic Lamp Wizards, Assassins, Cloaks of Invisibility, Martians, Zombies, Ninjas, Vampires, Psychics, Leprosy, Magicians, Chainsaws and more.

You collect the body parts by using attack cards and wildcards to steal them from other players, as well as picking them up from the deck. You lose body parts when other players attack you, though you can use defence cards and wildcards to stop other player's attacks.

Wildcards include the Magician card, where a player leaves the room and a card is hidden. They then have to find it and if they can't they lose 3 body parts.

The game takes roughly 20 minutes to play but gets quicker as it progresses as the number of body parts in the deck dwindles.

Each player can play as many cards as they want during their turn and ends their go when they pick up a card.

—description from the designer

In this press-your-luck party game, test how well you can predict what others will say at the table! Get points for matching answers that fall within a specific range of people-whoever gets the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Explore Xeo and discover buried riches. Collect money, keys and the coveted golden treasures! If Exis attacks, you could lose everything!


     Play Cards to explore map
     Reveal the buried surprise
     Don't let Exis steal your treasures


—description from the publisher

What can you see at first?

There are two coal armies lined up against each other. In the terrain where they move, every square obligates the characters to transform. When a character moves to a square of transformation, it has to change, but not only it’s appearance  will be different, but it’s ability to move and attack will be also changed.

3 characters, 2 teams, 1 battle

If I want to look for a similar game to Carbomorphozis, the first game that comes to my mind is the great, classic chess. Checkered board with 64 squares, 8 puppets, step and punch actions. However, while chess pieces are diverse with many features, we only have to count on 3 characters. And as long as everything is given in chess and victory depends solely on tactics, in Carbomorphozis the characters change from one step to another and this influences the outcome of the party and the applied military plan.

This board game is educational as well, because it combines three types of carbon in our minds for a lifetime. Thanks to the variety of the board, there are 256 different playing fields.

You can start the game on any side, giving you 1024 different play options, so the chance of repeating play is 1 to 1000, it means you won't get bored during the replays.

The playing time is relatively short, 20-30 minutes, so it is suitable for you if you have just little freetime. It doesn't need too much preparation, just take it out from the box and the game can begin. Another advantage is that it does not take place on too much space, so you can play it even while traveling by train.

I walk, I walk ... but what will happen to me when I stop?

The main driver of the game is transformation. Since it is a transformation of coal, the name of the game is the result of the fusion of the Latin name of carbon (carbon) and the Latin word for transformation (metamorphosis).

The pictograms on the game board symbolize a transformation. The given square determines what character should step on it in order that the carbomorphosis  can occur. It also specifies the new material structure.

Coal here or there… or is it already graphite?

There are three actions that you can take in the game: move, punch and transform. The first one and the second one are the player's decision, and the third one is the result of that decision. It is determined by the player’s move with the selected character or the square where the character is moved after the punch.

The purpose of the game is to beat all of your opponent's characters on the playing field. The more valuable our puppets are, the easier the task is for us and the harder for the opponent.

The game is as loose as the connection between the layers of graphite

What do we obtain with this game? The game primarily focuses on concentration and foresight, and therefore develops tactical skill. Because the rule is simple and easy to understand, the character's stepping and punching features are depicted on the characters themselves, and the transformations on the game board, so there is no need to learn the rules separately. The game can be started instantaneously and does not require lengthy learning.

As the game progresses, circumstances change so this board game is suitable for the more impatient children, too ; because it inspires them to make flexible decisions and modify easily  the previously planned steps.

If the players are children, the game can match to their forward thinking, which is only 1-2 steps. But nevertheless, it may require 4-5 step forward thinking from adult players, so the same game can be fun for all ages.

—description from the designer

Jan-Ken-Po (a Hawaiian name for Rock-Scissors-Paper) is a collectible card game with eighty cards in total, of which each box contains a random and poorly collated fifty. It is possible to get five of the same weapon in one set.  
 The game is set on Krone II (according to the box) or Pangea (according to the rulebook), where a centuries-old war has created three clans: the Paper Clan (yellow), the Scissors Clan (magenta) and the Stone Clan (blue). Most cards show badly drawn members of each clan and their weapons.

Players simultaneously reveal the top card on their deck, and stone cards beats scissors cards, scissors beat paper, and paper beats stone. With the exception of resolving ties and a 3-player version, that's the rules. Except there are rare monsters that beat all the clan cards, the wizard Jan-Ken-Po who beats everything, and a 'You Lose!' card ('the Herb of Destruction'), which must be played on yourself and makes you lose.

An attempt to cash in on the CCG boom of the mid 1990s, the game is noted for its low production values, amateurish art that is often printed sideways on the cards, a backstory that makes no sense, and gameplay that  takes Rock-Scissors-Paper and removes all the skill and tactics.

—user summary

boardgame: ドキドキ！自己紹介ゲーム (Doki Doki! Jiko Shokai Game)
description:
ドキドキ！自己紹介ゲーム (Doki Doki! Jiko Shokai Game) is a Japanese game for breaking the ice, designed to encourage social interaction between people meeting up for the first time. The players are required to tell some little story about themselves. But some of the players might be lying. So everyone has to work out who is telling the truth and who is lying. The game has twenty cards, two of which are lying cards. So a very large group can quickly have fun socialising, overcoming anxiety at being new in the group.

Dramatically introduce yourself, "There are people who know a lot"!

A scene of self-introduction at an event. About half are regulars or friends. The remark tends to be "Oh, I know everyone ...". This game is a self-introduction game that can help you get rid of those worries, make it easier for new players to enter, and familiarize you more!

ダラダラしがちな、「結構知ってる人がいる」自己紹介を劇的に！

１．ゲームの目的

boardgame: Moonstone: Gotchgut the Giant Monster Box
description:

Gotchgut is a giant - very strong, but not so fast being.

—description from the publisher

Slavisti is an abstract strategy game that comes with matches. It seems very simple at first glance, but is a game with strategic depth. It is designed as a non-commercial game.

Each player has two play areas on the table. In one area, only Roman numbers of 1s (I) 5s (V) and 10s (X) are formed with sticks. In the other area forms are laid. With the exception of the first stick, in the second area, sticks may only be laid at one end of a laying stick. So that both created an angle of 60, 90 or 180 degrees. It usually creates squares and equilateral triangles.
A special feature of the game is that a player can also lay sticks in the opposing areas. If you have laid down a stick in the enemy area, which has an open side, you may only lay there again, if you can lay a stick there, which is then closed at both ends. If that does not work, you can only lay down sticks in your own area.
When all the sticks have been laid, you can only lay sticks; never taking away the last laid wood. In the end, no stick may have an open end. 
The goal is to match the number of shapes (rectangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles) with the number of the Roman numeral.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ワードスナイパー (Word Sniper)
description:
ワードスナイパー (Word Sniper) is a Japanese speed word game. A deck of cards shows the start or end letters of words, and on the other side a topic or theme. The deck is shuffled and placed showing the theme side face-up. Then two cards are dealt to the table, flipped to show the characters. One letter to start the word, one to end the word. Players race to identify a word that fits the topic, and starts and ends accordingly. The first to get it right collects cards and scores points.

The publisher states that the shuffling of the deck will produce a large variety of combinations, keeping the game fresh to play with very simple rules. The publisher also says you can play the game with more players than shown on the box.

There were three subsequent editions published with new themes.
ワードスナイパーファミリー (Word Sniper Family) - with softer themes aimed at age 5+
ワードスナイパー・イマジン (Word Sniper Imagine) - with more abstract themes to encourage imaginative answers
ワードスナイパーエクストリーム (Word Sniper Extreme) - an expansion deck that adds more context to the themes, and has more letter forms on the flip side.

直感的で分かりやすい単純なルールなのでゲーム初心者でもすぐ始められます。 
カードをシャッフルすればお題と文字の組み合わせが変わるのでリプレイ性も高いです。 
プレイ時間も短く、推奨プレイ人数を超えた人数でもワイワイと遊ぶこともできる楽しいゲームです。

boardgame: Great War at Sea Jutland: Battle Analysis
description:
More ink has been spilled over the Battle of Jutland than any other naval battle in history. Jutland: Battle Analysis 1914 looks at what happened before the Battle of Jutland: Helgoland Bight, the Scarborough Raid, the Yarmouth Raid and more.
Jutland: Battle Analysis 1914' is different from other works on the North Sea campaign, thanks to its ties to our Great War at Sea: Jutland game. We look at how the game simulates the operations, and then we flesh them out with more battle scenarios – fast-playing actions using just the Tactical Map, where you fight out the battles between ships with guns and torpedoes.
Jutland, the game, has a lot of scenarios: 51 in total. Forty-four of those are operational scenarios, but only seven of them are battle scenarios. So the Battle Analysis adds 26 more of them, based on the surface actions that could have taken place – or at least one side hoped would take place – based on the expectations of one or both naval staffs when they sent the fleets to sea. Plus nine new operational scenarios.
Jutland is already a very fine game (our most popular ever, even more than Third Reich) and a good instrument through which to re-play history. The Battle Analysis makes it even more so, while adding more quick fun by fleshing out the battle scenario aspect of the game.
Jutland: Battle Analysis 1914 is a 64-page, large-format book supplement for Jutland; you’ll need a copy of the game to play most of the scenarios and Cruiser Warfare to play all of them.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
ダブルフェイス (Double Face) is a Japanese spy game.

One player is a spy, trying to leak secret information.

One player is an undercover investigator, who knows who the spy is and is trying to delete information before it leaks.

The other players are public security agents, trying to uncover the spy and the investigator.

Players have laptop computers with leaked info, deleted info, and dummy folders. The spy wins if enough info is leaked. The investigator wins if they complete four deletes. The public security staff win if they can stop the deletes.

But players can change roles during the game. A security agent may have to become a spy, or the spy might join the agency. In that case, who can you trust to share the information?

During the game, players may draw cards or put cards onto the table, or pass cards between them. If you can work out who is on your side, you can build a set of cards and satsify your win condition.

Bossbattle Bunnies is a short, fun card game for 3-6 players. In this game you embark on a 10-20 minute bunny-themed dungeon crawl with your friends. You'll find loot, get cursed, fight monsters, and most importantly, test your friendships. It's easy to learn the game as you go along, so grab a beer (or a lemonade) and go adventuring!

As if a pocket-size dungeon crawl wasn't awesome enough already, this game doubles as regular playing cards. That means you can use it for Poker, Solitaire, Go fish and all the other classics. Talk about value-for-money! You can even take it to the bar, do some tricks and sweep someone off their feet. Just be sure to send me an invite to the wedding if you do!

Bossbattle Bunnies originally had my own artstyle for consistency, but then I figured it would be way cooler to have many different artists (18 in total) contribute to this project in their own style. This resulted in 54 unique illustrations that together turn this 2-in-1 deck of cards into a portable museum!

—description from the designer

At last you can make the news! Bluff your way to the top and win fame and fortune by creating your own sensational mind-boggling Headlines. Play H.O.T., the wildly entertaining game that contains all the thrills of a roller coaster ride. H.O.T. is a hilarious, nail-biting adventure that gives you the chance to scoop incredible and outrageous Headlines of your own.

If you convince the other players that your phony headlines are authentic, then you can fib first to the finish to become a billionaire Tabloid Tycoon. Expose the lurid lifestyles of the rich and famous! Uncover sexy scandalous secrets! Investigate mysteries that defy the logic of science! Play H.O.T., the HEADLINES ON TABLOIDS game and experience an excitement you will never forget!!!

Bend Your Mind With Words in Disguise

Are you the kind of individual who enjoys solving personalized messages on car license plates? If so, Zoundz•Like will be an exciting and challenging game for you.

Players must solve the word of phrase disguised in the playword in less than 30 seconds.(Zoundz•Like is a lot more fun and easier to solve if you voice the playword out loud.) The first player or team to land exactly on the start/win space wins the game.

A die roll determines the play card number to be solved and the number of board game moves allowed. When a star appears behind a playword, the player must also give the correct spelling. Drive-By is a bonus turn with a 5 second time limit. Twisters are playwords which require mental and/or visual gymnastics to solve the word puzzle.

—description from the rulebook (summarized)

The Ludik Quebec Alice Board Game is a game of observation and speed for children 6 years and older. This game is designed with beautiful illustrations of the characters from the tale of Alice in Wonderland. The first player who finds the identical characters and exhausts all the cards in his deck as soon as possible wins the game! A simple and fun game that will appeal to your youngest!

Getting Ready: 

     A player shuffles the cards ad separates them into two equal piles
     Players sit in front of each other, take their pile of cards and put it face down in front of them
     Using the discs they make two columns of four discs each face up, leaving a space between columns large enough to contain four cards side by side.


Playing the game:
The game goes on until one of the players plays out his entire pile of cards.
Each turn is comprised of four steps, each one starting the same way
Players count to three, take the top card of their own pile and put it simultaneously in front of their opponents turned card. 
As soon as their cards are turned, players must be quick to locate identical cards. If there are no identical cards on the playing area, players move to the next step.

When there are two identical cards on the playing area, the first player to hit the disc matching the character on them taes a card from his own pile and covers one of the identical cards.
Players move on to the next step only when there are no identical cards on the playing area.

Note: Identical cards may be found in any column, not necessarily facing each other. In that case, players proceed in the same way. The player who is the quickest to hit the right disc covers one of the identical cards using a card from his own pile.

The first player to play the last card on his pile wins the game.

—user summary

Screwed . . . the card game where you're in it for yourself!

There are no partners!!!

The object of the game is to obtain the highest score by accurately predicting how many tricks you will take each round. The more you frustrate your opponents by taking their tricks or making them take tricks they do not want, the better your chances of winning will be.

Each round the strategy changes and you can try to bribe, beg, or sweet talk your opponents into helping you make your bid. If you fail, you can get revenge in the next round. Revenge is sweet! Players will find themselves with impetuous feelings of laughter, joy, hat, annoyance, excitement, or antagonism.

The trick to winning this challenging game is a mixture of skill and a lot of luck. No matter what your skill level, everyone has a chance to win this stimulating game.

Good luck . . . you'll need it!

—description from the back of the box

Contains six plastic screws with nuts used for bidding.

In Influencer, players take on the roles of wannabe influencers, chasing moments in real life not for the experience but instead for the social power of posting them later. Not every moment will prove to be popular though, as players watch the trending topics change round after round. Whoever can build their timeline in a way that attracts the most followers by the end of the game is the winner – though regardless of the outcome, the players may find out they’re all still wannabe influencers in the end!

Gameplay of Influencer is incredibly simple. Players collect cards resembling social media posts which have an image, a description, and different thematic categories (such as Gaming, Sports, Fashion, Health, Travel, etc.). Players draft cards from an open area each round with the final, unselected card becoming the trending topic (thematically this is to say that no matter what the players end up posting there is always something cooler than all of them… they are wannabe influencers after all!). After drafting players add a card from their hand to their feed (a row of cards in front of them). They then score based on chaining categories together (such as 3 Food posts in a row) and also earn points for matching the trending topic. As rounds proceed players remove cards from the end of their feed, placing them in their discard, making only their most relevant posts matter.

A casual, filler game, learned in minutes, Influencer is a lightweight experience that allows its designer, Peter Jürgensen, to play with humor and satire regarding a topic that many people can relate to.

—description from the publisher

In Fighting Chance choose one of four heroes to try and save the city. But everyone wants to be the best hero in the city, not only do you battle a collection of increasingly powerful rouges, you are trying to be a better hero than the heroes beside you.

Fighting Chance is a cooperative game of every person for themselves.

Fighting Chance is a game of three battle rounds to see who can become the strongest hero and save they City between two phases: training and battle.

Training Round 
Players can play two training cards from the training pile.  Follow any instructions on the training card. Put the used training cards in the training discard pile.  Players must discard their remaining training card.  At the beginning of the next training round players may draw three training cards again, but still play only two.  If there are not enough cards in the training card draw pile, shuffle the training card discard pile.  There is a training card called Diversify Your Portfolio, this card allows players to gain two of any attribute, but it may also be split between to attributes.

Battle Round 
Once the heroes are trained let the battle begin. 
Flip over the rouge for round 1.  Place the energy marker on the rogue board.
In additional rounds flip over round two’s rogue at the beginning of the battle round for round two, and round three’s rogue at the beginning of battle round three.
Note any special abilities that this rogue may have.  If a rogue is immune to solo attacks of level X, a player may play a solo attack if their skill exceeds the immunity.  For example if a rogue is immune to solo attacks of three or greater of mysticism, but the player has a level four or greater of mysticism, they may still attack at this level.  
The most experienced player should go first.
For the first round each player gets dealt seven cards, but has a hand size of three.  They players draw three cards in their hand, and draw up to three at the beginning of each turn.  If the player discards all their Battle Cards during a round, they are out of the round, but live to fight another day. 
For round two each player gets dealt eight cards with a hand size of four.
For round three each player gets dealt ten cards with a hand size of five.
At the end of each round place all unused cards in the discard pile.  Shuffle the Battle Cards and deal them for the next round.

They player with the most points at the end of round three wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heart of Crown: Fairy Garden – Advanced Play Mini Expansion
description:
Are you a Heart of Crown expert? Get to the action faster by swapping out your starting deck in this mini expansion. Compatible with all the Heart of Crown games - the base game is required (original Heart of Crown or Fairy Garden).

In the Advanced Play Mini Expansion, each player starts the game with a deck of 10 cards as listed below. Otherwise, the rules of the game are unchanged.

6 Farming Villages
1 Mansion (New)
1 Attendant (New)
1 Alternate Art Apprentice Maid
1 Personal Maid (New)

To begin the game, instead of the number of Apprentice Maids, determine who goes first by who has the highest total number of Attendants, Apprentice Maids, and Personal Maids. All four Personal Maids have the same effect.

Number of Cards Included: 16
Sleeve Size: Standard

—description from the publisher

As Haggatha the Oracle, you deal in fortunes for the unfortunate. Read the stars, divine the future, and twist the fates in your attempts to part folks from their precious gold. Weary adventurers visit your shop in search of hope and guidance. You offer this through lucky charms and horoscopes, but sometimes you need to do a little voodoo, too. Just be careful not to let your dark dabblings—or dirty dishes—scare away too many honest customers!

The Oracle is a new merchant you can play as. It is centered around a roll & write style game, with a pen, dry-erase board, and custom dice tray. Each turn you throw a handful of runes (a coin, bone, voodoo meeple, and dice) into a divided dish, then you record the results on your board in various ways to perform actions—and predict the future!

—description from the publisher

description:
小魚爭10 (Add Fish Up To 10)

The first game in its series on fun math boardgames. An educational math game for 2-5 players of aged 4 or above. A mechanism similar to blackjack where you push your luck to catch more fish without getting busted of more than 10 fish. Young players can learn simple additions and mathematics elementary through playing this game. It is a family friend game with 65 cards in a tin case.

The second game in its series on fun math boardgames. A educational math game for 2-4 players of aged 4 or above. It is a ladder climbing card game with cards of 1 to 4 cats. The objective is to get rid of all hand cards. Young players can learn simple additions of card combinations through playing this game. It is a family friend game with 50 cards in a tin case.
